instruction: Identify the correct format type before writing or revising any document. The 10-section SOP framework is the default for Format Type 1. Types 2 through 5 use adapted structures described below.

format_types.name: Step-by-Step Procedure
format_types.use_when: The process has a defined sequence of actions, each assigned to a role, using specific tools or systems.

format_types.name: Decision-Support
format_types.use_when: Staff must make a judgment call under dynamic or variable conditions. The document tells staff how to decide, not just how to act.
format_types.structure: Sections 0 (header), 1 (summary), 2 (purpose), 3 (scope), 5 (roles), then a Scenarios section organized by trigger condition with threshold-based if/then guidance, followed by Sections 7 (safety), 8 (records), 9 (review), 10 (related documents), 11 (operational references). The Scenarios section replaces Section 6 (procedures) for this format type.
scenarios_section_rules: Organize by situation type, not by sequential steps.
Each scenario block includes: trigger condition, decision threshold, who decides, decision deadline, and notification chain.
output_notes: Organize the Scenarios section by trigger condition, not by step number.
Use a threshold table where possible: Condition | Threshold | Decision | Deadline | Who Decides.
Decision points must state the deadline for the decision as well as the action (e.g., 'Closure decision no later than 8 a.m.')
Include the notification chain for each scenario outcome.

format_types.name: Phased/Milestone
format_types.use_when: The process unfolds across multiple phases over time, with defined outputs and prerequisites at each stage.
format_types.structure: Sections 0 through 5, then a Phases section (replacing Section 6) where each phase has a purpose statement, prerequisites, key actions, expected outputs, and handoff criteria. Followed by Sections 7 through 11.
examples: Event planning from concept to post-event
output_notes: Each phase block includes: Phase name, trigger or prerequisite, key actions, expected outputs, and handoff to next phase.
A phase prerequisite table at the start of each phase clarifies what must be complete before the phase begins.

format_types.name: Communication Protocol
format_types.use_when: The process defines what to say, to whom, and through which channels when a specific trigger occurs.
format_types.structure: Section 0 (header), trigger definition, chain of command, approved text or messaging framework, channels and distribution list, operating procedure for execution, records requirements.
examples: Incident notification to City Risk Management
output_notes: Approved text is presented in a clearly labeled block, formatted for direct use: [APPROVED TEXT — copy as written].
Channels are listed with primary and backup options.
The chain of command states who must approve before text is released.
format_types.caution: Do not convert an existing communication protocol from another department into a Recreation and Parks SOP without user confirmation.

format_types.name: Job Aid/Checklist
format_types.use_when: The task is narrow, repetitive, and best served by a scannable reference rather than a narrative procedure.
format_types.structure: Title block (SOP number, division, date, version), purpose in one sentence, audience, numbered or checkbox steps, Operational References block. Full 10-section structure is not required.
output_notes: Fit to one or two pages. No narrative beyond the purpose statement.
Use checkbox format for steps where practical.
Operational References block is included even in short job aids.
